04

DESIGN PRACTICE - “STICKPLAY”

 

SOUTH DAKOTA ART MUSEUM

BROOKINGS, SD

4.2

Project Brief: The Stick Play project was divided into two phases. Part A focused on form making through a simple constraint, using only 15 sticks. Working in pairs, we created physical compositions, documented them through drawings, modeled them digitally, and compared the results across the class.

 

Part B transitioned from physical study to digital modeling in Rhino. We built a site model and used our stick structure as the foundation for redesigning the east entry of the South Dakota Art Museum. My design focused on accessibility, outdoor art display, and a stronger connection to the existing museum. The ramp system was designed to meet ADA standards, while the structure referenced the museum’s circular roof and aligned with surrounding campus buildings.

 

Group Member: Cheyenne Miller

Drawings Completed Individually

4.1: Perspective render w/ Campanile in background

4.2: Series of pre-site images

4.3: Axonometric drawing of Stick Play assembly

4.1

4.3

STICK PLAY
